name: Clearside Biomedical, Inc.
business: The company sells a proprietary suprachoroidal injection platform (SCS Microinjector) for targeted delivery of therapies to the back of the eye, and its first product XIPERE is approved for macular edema associated with uveitis. It develops internal pipeline candidates such as CLS-AX for wet AMD and partners with other companies to use its platform for their drug candidates.

exposures.sentence: Our first product, XIPERE (triamcinolone acetonide injectable suspension) for suprachoroidal use, was approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration, or the FDA, in October 2021.

Our novel SCS injection platform, utilizing our proprietary SCS Microinjector, enables an in-office, repeatable, non-surgical procedure for the targeted and compartmentalized delivery of a wide variety of therapies to the macula, retina or choroid to potentially preserve and improve vision in patients with sight-threatening eye diseases.
Our most advanced clinical development product candidate is a proprietary suspension of axitinib, a tyrosine kinase inhibitor, or TKI, for suprachoroidal injection, which we refer to as CLS-AX.
We believe suprachoroidal administration of gene therapy could ultimately enhance access to care because it does not require specialized gene therapy surgery treatment centers.

catalysts.event: End-of-Phase 2 meeting with FDA for CLS-AX
catalysts.sentence: We conducted an End-of-Phase 2 meeting with the FDA in the first quarter of 2025 and gained alignment on the essential components of our Phase 3 program.

catalysts.event: Initiate Phase 3 clinical trials for CLS-AX
catalysts.sentence: We are preparing to be ready to initiate both clinical trials in the second half of 2025, subject to our ability to pay for the trials.

catalysts.event: BioCryst plans to advance avoralstat into a clinical trial for DME
catalysts.sentence: In February 2025, BioCryst announced that it plans to advance avoralstat into a clinical trial of patients with DME by the end of 2025.

catalysts.event: REGENXBIO plans to start Phase 3 program for sura-vec in DR
catalysts.sentence: In January 2025, REGENXBIO announced that a global Phase 3 clinical program in DR for their gene therapy ABBV-RGX-314, now referred to as Sura-vec, is planned to start later in 2025.

catalysts.event: Arctic Vision NDA approvals for ARCATUS in Australia and Singapore
catalysts.sentence: In January 2025, Arctic Vision announced that its NDAs for ARCATUS for the treatment of uveitic macular edema were approved by the Therapeutic Goods Administration of Australia and the Health Sciences Authority in Singapore.

catalysts.event: Arctic Vision NDA accepted for review in China
catalysts.sentence: In February 2025, Arctic Vision announced its NDA for ARCATUS for the treatment of uveitic macular edema was formally accepted for review by the Center for Drug Evaluation of China National Medical Products Administration.

catalysts.event: Permanent Category 1 CPT code granted for suprachoroidal injection
catalysts.sentence: On January 1, 2024, a permanent Category 1 Current Procedural Terminology, or CPT, code was granted for the suprachoroidal injection of pharmacologic agents.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 20 million individuals in the United States aged 40 and older are affected with AMD, with a global prevalence of 170 million.
tamClaims.sentence: Approximately 20 million individuals in the United States aged 40 and older are affected with AMD, with a global prevalence of 170 million.

tamClaims.claim: An estimated 10% to 15% of people with AMD will develop the wet form.
tamClaims.sentence: An estimated 10% to 15% of people with AMD will develop the wet form, which refers to the advanced neovascular stage of the disease in which blood vessels leak blood and fluid into the macula and damage photoreceptor cells.

tamClaims.claim: Current anti-VEGF market generated aggregate 2020 sales of approximately $14.3 billion globally.
tamClaims.sentence: The current anti-VEGF market for the treatment of retinal diseases consists of several drugs that generated aggregate 2020 sales of approximately $14.3 billion globally.

tamClaims.claim: Uveitis affects approximately 350,000 patients in the United States and more than one million worldwide.
tamClaims.sentence: Uveitis is a set of ocular inflammatory conditions affecting approximately 350,000 patients in the United States and more than one million worldwide.

tamClaims.claim: Approximately one-third of uveitis patients develop uveitic macular edema.
tamClaims.sentence: Approximately one-third of uveitis patients develop uveitic macular edema, a build-up of fluid in the macula, the area of the retina responsible for sharp, straight-ahead vision.

tamClaims.claim: Choroidal melanoma incidence approximately 11,000 patients per year in the United States and Europe.
tamClaims.sentence: Choroidal melanoma is the most common intraocular cancer in adults, with an incidence of approximately 11,000 patients per year in the United States and Europe.
